title: MCP Trust Registry — Attestation
description: Schema for a signed attestation binding a manifest hash to an artifact hash, per protocol v0.1 §6. Signature is over the canonical form with the signature and sigstore_bundle fields removed (§6.2). Either 'signature' or 'sigstore_bundle' MUST be non-null; keyless attestations populate the bundle (§6.3).

manifest_hash.description: Canonical hash of the manifest this attestation binds. Format: 'sha256:<hex>'.

artifact_hash.description: Hash of the on-disk artifact. Format: 'sha256:<hex>'. Hashing convention per manifest.artifact.type (§4.5).

expires_at.description: Expiry timestamp. MUST be no more than 18 months after issued_at for non-keyless attestations. Keyless attestations MAY set this to null.

signature.description: ed25519 signature over the canonical form of this document with 'signature' and 'sigstore_bundle' removed. Set to null only when keyless (sigstore_bundle MUST then be non-null).

sigstore_bundle.description: Sigstore bundle for keyless signing. When non-null, the bundle MUST chain to the manifest's publisher.oidc_subject and publisher.oidc_issuer.

ed25519_signature.description: ed25519 signature encoded as 'ed25519:' + standard base64 (with padding) of the 64-byte signature.

sigstore_bundle.description: Sigstore protobuf bundle, JSON-serialized. Schema follows the Sigstore project; verifier uses Sigstore tooling for validation rather than re-validating here.
